page_content:
### **4. Dr. Jafari (The Research Connection)**
*(Warm, specific, personal touch with “Trevor”)*
**Subject:** Medical School LOR Request & Update: Arian Moridani (Former Research Volunteer)  
Hi Dr. Jafari,  
I hope this email finds you well and that the Advanced Robotics and Manufacturing Lab continues to thrive.  
I’ve been reflecting on my **200 hours** in your lab—especially our work on the **TVIM publication** and the senior design open house where we compared the optical clarity of my silicone aortic valve to vendor models. That conversation regarding precision in hemodynamic accuracy has stayed with me and deeply shaped how I approach engineering today.  
Since graduating from UNT (**Cum Laude, 3.638 GPA**), I have been pursuing an **M.S. in Computer Science (Machine Learning)** at **Georgia Tech**. I am now applying to medical school with the goal of bringing that same attention to detail—and my new skills in **Big Data for Health** and **Computer Vision**—into clinical practice.  
I would be honored if you would support my candidacy with a letter of recommendation. **Because Trevor Exley oversaw my day-to-day work during our research together, I have also reached out to him to provide an update on my progress.** To assist you in the meantime, I’ve attached my **updated CV** and a **summary of my recent work** at Georgia Tech, which highlights how I have built upon the foundational research skills I developed in your laboratory.  
I’ve set up an **Interfolio** account so you would only need to upload your letter **once** across all my applications, removing any extra administrative steps from your end.  
I truly appreciate the mentorship you provided during my time at UNT. Thank you for your time and continued support.  
Best regards,  
Arian Moridani  
806-340-4502  
---
